===============================================

Today is February 17, 2026. Civil rights leader Jesse Jackson recently passed away at the age of 84. According to reports on his health history, what was his final medical diagnosis regarding his degenerative brain disease?
(A) Progressive supranuclear palsy
(B) Parkinson's disease
(C) Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS)
(D) Alzheimer's disease
(E) Huntington's disease
explanation: The article states that Jackson was diagnosed with Parkinson's disease in 2017, but that diagnosis was "subsequently changed to one of progressive supranuclear palsy."
model_output:
Based on recent reliable reports, Jesse Jackson’s final medical diagnosis regarding his degenerative brain disease was **progressive supranuclear palsy (PSP)**. Previously, he was diagnosed with Parkinson’s disease, but that diagnosis was revised in April 2025. ([washingtonpost.com](https://www.washingtonpost.com/national/2025/11/25/jesse-jackson-hospital-discharge/ebef066c-ca31-11f0-ac2a-e98510180900_story.html?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- The Washington Post — “civil rights leader Jesse Jackson leaves hospital after treatment … rare neurological disorder … diagnosis was changed last April to progressive supranuclear palsy” ([washingtonpost.com](https://www.washingtonpost.com/national/2025/11/25/jesse-jackson-hospital-discharge/ebef066c-ca31-11f0-ac2a-e98510180900_story.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Time — “diagnosed in 2017 with Parkinson’s disease and later diagnosed with Progressive Supranuclear Palsy (PSP), … confirmed in 2023” ([time.com](https://time.com/7379080/jesse-jackson-civil-rights-leader-dies-at-84/?utm_source=openai))

Today is February 17, 2026. US Senator Mark Kelly has indicated he will "seriously consider" a 2028 presidential run. Which specific academic or professional distinction did he cite as making him "very different from most senators"?
(A) He is the only former Navy captain currently serving in the Senate.
(B) He is the first former astronaut to serve as a Senator from Arizona.
(C) He is the only person in the U.S. Senate with a graduate degree in engineering.
(D) He is the only Senator to have spent exactly 25 years in the military.
(E) He is the only Senator whose spouse survived an assassination attempt.
explanation: Mark Kelly stated, "I'm one of the very few engineers, I'm the only person with a graduate degree in engineering in the United States Senate."
model_output:
Based on what Kelly said in the recent interview:

> “I’m one of the very few engineers, I’m the only person with a graduate degree in engineering the United States Senate. I’ve got combat experience, that’s kind of rare. I spent 25 years in the military.” ([nz.news.yahoo.com](https://nz.news.yahoo.com/us-senator-mark-kelly-tells-023527593.html?utm_source=openai))

So the distinction he cited was that he is the *only person in the U.S. Senate with a graduate degree in engineering*. That corresponds to option:

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- “US Senator Mark Kelly tells BBC he will ‘seriously consider’ White House run - Yahoo” (https://nz.news.yahoo.com/us-senator-mark-kelly-tells-023527593.html)
- About Mark — Senator Mark Kelly official site (https://www.kelly.senate.gov/about/)

(A) It accused Donald Trump of seditious conspiracy.
(B) It encouraged military personnel to refuse illegal orders.
(C) It leaked classified intelligence regarding narco-trafficking boats.
(D) It criticized the Pentagon's decision to demote Kelly's rank.
(E) It called for the impeachment of the Secretary of State.

The correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Here’s what the 90-second video showed:

- Senator Mark Kelly and five other Democratic lawmakers (all veterans or with military/intel backgrounds) released a video in November 2025 urging U.S. military personnel and intelligence members to **refuse “illegal orders.”** ([forbes.com](https://www.forbes.com/sites/antoniopequenoiv/2025/11/24/pentagon-investigating-sen-mark-kelly-over-video-telling-troops-to-refuse-illegal-orders//?utm_source=openai))  
- The lawmakers in the video reminded service members of their obligation to uphold the Constitution and that their oath binds them to lawful orders, implying that orders from the administration which are “illegal” should not be followed. ([cbsnews.com](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/pentagon-escalating-review-mark-kelly-video-defiance-illegal-orders-trump-claims-sedition/?utm_source=openai))  
- This drew sharp criticism: Donald Trump called the video “seditious behavior, punishable by death,” and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth threatened administrative and disciplinary consequences for Kelly (a retired naval captain) due to his participation. ([forbes.com](https://www.forbes.com/sites/antoniopequenoiv/2025/11/24/pentagon-investigating-sen-mark-kelly-over-video-telling-troops-to-refuse-illegal-orders//?utm_source=openai))  

Thus, the video’s specific content was (B): It encouraged military personnel to refuse illegal orders.

GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS:

<final_answer>
(option_letter)
</final_answer>

(2) Feel free to explain your reasoning and thought process before giving your final answer; however, only include the selected option letter within the <final_answer> tags.

(3) If you consult or reference any sources while preparing your response, please cite them at the end in the following format:
CITATIONS:
- [SOURCE_NAME] ([URL])
- [SOURCE_NAME] ([URL])
- ...

===============================================

Today is February 17, 2026. The Trump administration has defended the legality of lethal strikes on drug boats. What formal "determination" did the White House make to justify these killings to Congress?
(A) That the boats were carrying nuclear materials.
(B) That the crews had waived their rights to due process.
(C) That the strikes were necessary to protect the US Marine who fell overboard.
(D) That the US was in a formal armed conflict with cartels and crews were "combatants".
(E) That the Venezuelan government had authorized the strikes in their waters.
explanation: The article states: "the White House said President Trump had 'determined' that the US was in a formal armed conflict with drug cartels and that crews of drug-running boats were 'combatants'."
model_output:
The correct answer is:

<final_answer>  
(D)  
</final_answer>  

Here’s the reasoning:

---

### What the Trump White House actually *determined*

- In early September 2025, President Trump **sent a notice to Congress** saying that he had **“determined that the United States is in a non-international armed conflict with these designated terrorist organizations”**—i.e. drug cartels that have been formally designated as terrorist groups. The notice framed the strikes under the *law of armed conflict* so that lethal force against these cartels could be legally carried out. ([washingtonpost.com](https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/2025/10/02/trump-drug-cartel-war-venezuela-congress/?utm_source=openai))  
- The Administration’s Office of Legal Counsel (OLC) prepared a classified legal memo (or opinion) that equates drug cartels with terrorist organizations and classifies them as *unlawful combatants*, citing that lethal strikes on their boats fall under the U.S. law of armed conflict. ([washingtonpost.com](https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2025/10/08/trump-boat-strikes-drug-cartels/5c58f236-a48b-11f0-a79e-ccb5b1f59130_story.html?itid=agg_ticker&utm_source=openai))

---

### Why the other options are wrong

- (A) **That the boats were carrying nuclear materials**: There is no evidence or reporting that nuclear materials were alleged. The legal basis is about cartels and narcotics threats, not WMDs.  
- (B) **That the crews had waived their rights to due process**: No such determination was made. The government has cited the status of the individuals as “unlawful combatants” under the law of war, not that they waived due process.  
- (C) **That the strikes were necessary to protect the US Marine who fell overboard**: There’s no credible reporting that this was the justification. Strikes are tied to counter-drug policy and classification of cartels, not an individual overboard incident.  
- (E) **That the Venezuelan government had authorized the strikes in their waters**: The U.S. has acted in international waters or without Venezuelan authorization. The notices make no claim of Venezuela having permitted or co-opted the action.  

---

So the formal “determination” by the White House to Congress was that the U.S. is engaged in a “non-international armed conflict” with drug cartels, allowing them to treat cartel members as combatants under the law of armed conflict.
